# ngx-datatables-net

> Modern Angular wrapper for DataTables.net. It lets you use DataTables (sorting, search,
> pagination, and the full set of DataTables extensions) in Angular 20, 21 and 22 through a
> directive driven by signals, with no jQuery in your own code. Maintained by Ascentspark.

ngx-datatables-net is published on npm as `ngx-datatables-net`. You add the `[dtTable]` directive
to a native `<table>`, bind `dtData` and `dtColumns` as Angular inputs, and read events and state
back as signals. It is standalone, zoneless-ready and SSR-safe, and ships four styling adapters:
the DataTables default (dt), Bootstrap 5, Tailwind, and Material.

It also provides free double-click edit-in-place via the `[dtEditable]` directive: inline cell
editing (text, textarea, number, date, checkbox, select, multiselect and custom editors) with
validation and optional async saving. This is a free, MIT-licensed alternative to inline editing
from DataTables Editor, which is a paid commercial extension.

## Documentation

- [Getting started](https://ngx-datatables-net.ascentspark.com/): install, configure and a copy-paste first table.
- [Live examples](https://ngx-datatables-net.ascentspark.com/): data sourcing, features, styling adapters, and every DataTables extension.
- [Edit in place demo](https://ngx-datatables-net.ascentspark.com/features/edit-in-place): double-click inline editing for every editor type.
- [Edit in place guide](https://github.com/ascentspark/ngx-datatables-net/blob/main/docs/EDIT-IN-PLACE.md): the `[dtEditable]` directive in full.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ascentspark/ngx-datatables-net): source, README, architecture and security notes.
- [DataTables.net](https://datatables.net): the underlying table library this package wraps.

## Key facts

- Install: `npm install ngx-datatables-net datatables.net datatables.net-dt`
- Selector: attribute directive `[dtTable]` on a `<table>` element.
- Inputs: `dtOptions` (DataTables Config), `dtData` (row array), `dtColumns` (column definitions).
- Outputs: `dtInit`, `dtDraw`, `dtPage`, `dtXhr`, `dtSelect`, `dtDeselect`, `dtRowClick`.
- Edit in place: add the `[dtEditable]` directive and give a column an `editor` (`type` of text, textarea, number, date, checkbox, select, multiselect or custom). Bind `[dtSave]` for sync/Promise/Observable saving; outputs `dtCellEditStart`, `dtCellEdit`, `dtCellEditCancel`, `dtCellEditError`. Free and MIT, no DataTables Editor licence needed. Added in 22.2.0.
- Reload data by assigning a new array to the `dtData` signal; no manual redraw trigger.
- Escaped output by default via `withSafeDefaults()`; opt into HTML with a DomSanitizer-backed renderer.
- One package major per Angular major: `22.x` (latest), `21.x` (ng21), `20.x` (ng20).
- License: MIT, by Ascentspark (https://ascentspark.com).

## FAQ

- Does it require jQuery? You never write jQuery; the library uses DataTables' non-jQuery API. jQuery remains a transitive dependency of datatables.net but never appears in your code.
- Which Angular versions? Angular 20, 21 and 22, one package major each (22.x / 21.x / 20.x).
- How to reload data? Assign a new array to the dtData signal; the table reconciles automatically, no manual trigger.
- NgModules or Zone.js? Neither. Standalone directive, zoneless-ready (and works in zoned apps too).
- XSS safety? withSafeDefaults() escapes columns without a renderer; opt into HTML via the DomSanitizer-backed renderer.
- Ajax / server-side? Yes, via the ajax option (string, object, or function) and serverSide: true.
- Extensions? All official DataTables extensions work through pass-through dtOptions; the commercial Editor extension is also compatible via the exposed Api.
- Free inline editing / alternative to the paid DataTables Editor? Yes. The `[dtEditable]` directive gives free, MIT-licensed double-click edit-in-place (text, textarea, number, date, checkbox, select, multiselect and custom editors, validation and async saving) without the commercial Editor licence. Added in 22.2.0.
- SSR? Yes, the table initialises in the browser only (afterNextRender).
